info.title: Tightrope Tracker Public API
info.summary: Live, transparent dashboard of the UK growth agenda's real constraints.
info.description:
Tightrope Tracker exposes the same normalised scoring data that drives the public dashboard at https://tightropetracker.uk. All endpoints are read-only JSON, CORS-open, and cached briefly at the edge. Every number ultimately traces back to a named public publisher (Bank of England, ONS, OBR, DMO, MHCLG, parliament.uk). Numbers can be verified against the `sourceId` field on each contribution and cross-checked on the /methodology page of the site.

The API is provided under the MIT licence of the underlying code, with no warranty. Primary-source data retains its original publisher's licence; see the /sources page of the site for per-source terms.

Rate limit: 120 requests / 60s / client IP. Exceeding returns `429` with `Retry-After`, `X-RateLimit-Limit` and `X-RateLimit-Remaining` headers. The `/api/v1/health` endpoint is exempt.

tags.name: Delivery
tags.description: Curated delivery commitments tracked against published targets.

tags.name: Timeline
tags.description: Editorial timeline of events that moved the score.

tags.name: Lookup
tags.description: Utility lookups — e.g. MP by postcode.

tags.name: Meta
tags.description: Health, spec, and other meta endpoints.

get.description: Returns the most recent recompute of the headline score, all four pillar scores, and — when any source has recently failed — a list of affected sources. Cached at the edge with a 30-minute freshness guard.
Daily-granularity history of headline + pillar scores over the requested window. The default `days=90` slice is served from an edge cache; other windows are computed on demand.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Window length in days (inclusive of today). Minimum 1, maximum 800.

200.description: History points, ordered by timestamp ascending.

get.description: Editorial scorecard of specific growth-agenda commitments (new towns, SMR fleet, Industrial Strategy milestones, housing trajectory, etc.) with status, source label, and a link back to the primary announcement.

200.description: All tracked commitments, sorted by editorial `sort_order` then name.

get.description: Reverse-chronological list of events (monetary, fiscal, policy, geopolitical, market, delivery) with short editorial summaries and, where meaningful, an attributed score delta. The default window (`limit=40`) is served from an edge cache; custom limits are computed on demand.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum events to return. Minimum 1, maximum 200.

get.summary: Look up a Member of Parliament by postcode
get.description: Resolves a UK postcode to the current MP via the parliament.uk Members API. Results are cached per outward code (e.g. `SW1A`) for 7 days. The response includes the MP's parliamentary email when the upstream contacts endpoint exposes one; otherwise the canonical `firstname.lastname.mp@parliament.uk` form is derived. Use `profileUrl` for the MP's official contact page.

parameters.name: postcode
parameters.description: UK postcode. Accepts with or without the internal space. Case-insensitive.

200.description: MP currently representing the constituency that contains this postcode.

400.description: Missing, malformed, or unknown query parameter.

404.description: No MP found for the given postcode (valid shape, no match upstream).

get.description: Cheap health signal. Returns `ok: true` when the DB is reachable and includes the last-success timestamp of every ingestion source so operators can chart feed lag. Returns `ok: false` with HTTP 503 if the audit table can't be read. Exempt from rate limiting.

503.description: Degraded — audit table unreachable. Not rate-limited.

get.description: Serves this document in its canonical form. The web app serves the same content at https://tightropetracker.uk/openapi.json and renders it interactively at https://tightropetracker.uk/docs.

Compact quantile summaries (101 knots) of every indicator's historical baseline window (2019-present minus the 2020 COVID outlier). Designed for in-browser use by the /explore what-if simulator so it can reproduce the live methodology's empirical-CDF normalisation without shipping the full multi-thousand-sample baseline. Cached at the edge for 24 hours.

500.description: Could not assemble baselines from D1.

CacheControl.description: Edge + browser cache hints. Errors override to `no-store`.

RetryAfter.description: Seconds until the rate-limit window resets.

RateLimitLimit.description: Maximum requests per window for this client IP.

RateLimitRemaining.description: Requests remaining in the current window. Always `0` on a 429.

BadQuery.description: One or more query parameters were unknown or out of range.

example.error: days must be an integer between 1 and 365

NotSeeded.description: The underlying data hasn't been ingested yet. Distinct from a 500 `INTERNAL` failure — the database is up; there is simply no data to return. Note the response body uses `message` rather than `error`.

example.message: Data has not been ingested yet

PillarId.description: The four scored pillars of the Tightrope index.

Trend.description: Direction of a trend arrow. `up`/`down`/`flat` is semantically neutral: whether up is good or bad depends on the indicator's `risingIsBad` flag (see /methodology).

ScoreBand.description: Qualitative band for a score in [0,100]. Since schema v2, lower scores are worse. Thresholds: critical 0–20, acute 20–40, strained 40–60, steady 60–80, slack 80–100.

DeliveryStatus.description: Editorial assessment of a delivery commitment against its stated target.

IndicatorContribution.description:
One indicator's contribution to its pillar score. `normalised` uses the public score direction: 0 = critical / badly off track, 100 = on track / room to move. Higher is better.

Note: on a cache miss, `zScore` and `normalised` may be approximate placeholders until the full snapshot is rebuilt. `rawValue`, `observedAt`, `sourceId`, and `weight` are always authoritative.

weight.description: Intra-pillar weight, normalised so siblings sum to 1.

value.description: 0 = critical / badly off track, 100 = on track / room to move. Higher is better.

delta7d.description: Change in score points vs ~7 days ago.

delta30d.description: Change in score points across the full `sparkline30d` window (first → last).

sparkline30d.description: Up to 30 values, one per UTC day, oldest first.

stale.description: Present and `true` iff fewer than a quorum of indicators have a fresh reading; the value is a last-known carry, not a fresh recompute. Omitted otherwise.

value.description: 0 = critical / badly off track, 100 = on track / room to move. Higher is better.

editorial.description: Editorial one-liner chosen to match the current band.

delta24h.description: Headline delta across the most recent one-UTC-day step of the scored series. Historically named for API stability; labelled `1d` in the UI.

delta30dBaselineDate.description: ISO date of the row actually used as the 30d baseline. Populated only when the baseline sits meaningfully off a clean 30 days (typically because history doesn't reach back far enough).

deltaYtd.description: Headline delta since 1 January of the current year.

deltaYtdBaselineDate.description: ISO date of the row actually used as the YTD baseline. Populated when the baseline is later than Jan 1, or when it collapsed onto the 30d baseline row.

dominantPillar.description: The weighted pillar shortfall contributing the largest drag to the current score.

sparkline90d.description: Up to 90 values, one per UTC day, oldest first.

stale.description: Present and `true` iff any pillar was flagged stale. Consumers should show a "stale data" chip and avoid treating the headline as authoritative.

SourceHealthEntry.description: A source whose most recent ingestion attempt did not succeed. Surfaces upstream outages before the staleness thresholds on pillars/headline fire.

sourceHealth.description: Sources whose latest ingestion attempt did not succeed. Absent or empty when every source is healthy.

scoreDirection.description: Public score polarity: 100 = room to move / on track, 0 = critical / badly off track. A falling value means conditions are worsening.

rangeDays.description: Maximum range returned. Older data is available in the site's historical archives.

scoreDirection.description: Public score polarity for every value in this series.

latest.description: Latest observed figure, formatted for display.

scoreDelta.description: Score points attributed to this event, signed. Present only when we have enough confidence to attribute it.

email.description: Parliamentary email address for the MP. Sourced from the parliament.uk Members API contact endpoint where present (`type === "Parliamentary"`); when the upstream omits it, derived as `firstname.lastname.mp@parliament.uk` — the canonical form for a sitting MP. May be `null` only when neither path can resolve a value (e.g. unusual member naming).

ingestionLastSuccess.description: Map of `sourceId` → ISO timestamp of the last successful ingest for that source.
Empty object when the audit table is unreachable.

error.description: Human-readable description. Content is not part of the stable API contract — clients should dispatch on `code`.

code.description: Stable machine-readable error code. Clients should switch on this.

NotSeededError.description: The `NOT_SEEDED` 503 uses `message` rather than `error` — historical wart preserved for clients that already handle it.
